name: Apple Crumb Coffeecake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 2-quart saucepan, combine half cup sugar, cornstarch, cinnamon, and nutmeg. Add corn syrup, apples, and currants. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently until the mixture thickens. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine flour and remaining three quarters cup sugar. Using a pastry blender, cut in margarine until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Remove half of this crumb mixture and set aside for topping.
3. To the remaining mixture, stir salt, baking powder, and baking soda. Mix egg and buttermilk together. Add dry ingredients to the egg-buttermilk mixture, stirring only until combined.
4. Spray a 9-inch round cake pan with vegetable shortening spray. Spread two thirds of the batter over the bottom of the pan. Spread two thirds of the apple mixture over the batter. Dot with remaining batter and apple mixture, spreading evenly. Sprinkle with reserved crumb mixture.
5. Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes. Cool slightly before serving.
